posts.caption:
Jacob & Co. continues to blur the line between watchmaking, sculpture, and fantasy with the Astronomia “Gates of Olympus.”
Priced at $1.4 million, the one-of-one creation draws inspiration from Greek mythology, featuring Zeus, Olympus, celestial movement, and a fully mechanical miniature world built directly into the timepiece itself.
Inside the 18K rose gold case sits a triple-axis tourbillon, over 9 carats of baguette diamonds, and an intricate Olympus scene designed as part of the movement.
More than a watch, the piece reflects how modern haute horology is increasingly moving toward collectible art and immersive storytelling.
@JacobAndCo #JacobAndCo #Luxurious #Collab

posts.caption:
Ferrari has unveiled a special Monaco Grand Prix race suit for Charles Leclerc, giving the hometown hero a unique look for Formula 1’s most iconic race weekend.
Departing from Ferrari’s traditional red, the one-off suit features a predominantly white design accented with red details inspired by the colours of Monaco’s national flag. The special livery celebrates Leclerc’s deep connection to the Principality, where he grew up and first developed his passion for racing.
The reveal has already generated significant attention among Formula 1 fans, with many praising the elegant Monaco-inspired design and calling for Ferrari to give its race car a matching special livery for the event.
As Leclerc returns to the streets he knows better than anyone else, the special suit adds another personal touch to one of the most anticipated races on the Formula 1 calendar.

posts.caption:
Lamborghini has delivered the first Temerario in India, marking the beginning of a new hybrid era for the Italian supercar maker in one of its fastest-growing markets. The Temerario succeeds the iconic Huracán and becomes Lamborghini’s latest electrified super sports car on Indian roads.
At its heart is an all-new 4.0-litre twin-turbo V8 paired with three electric motors, producing a combined 920hp. The hybrid powertrain enables a 0-100 km/h sprint in just 2.7 seconds and a top speed exceeding 340 km/h, making it one of the most advanced performance cars Lamborghini has ever built.
The delivery represents more than the arrival of a new model. It signals Lamborghini’s transition from the naturally aspirated V10 era of the Huracán to a future centred around high-performance hybrid technology, while continuing to push the limits of speed, engineering, and driving emotion.
📸: @lamborghini

posts.caption:
Taj has officially entered Continental Europe with the opening of Taj Hessischer Hof Frankfurt, marking a major milestone in the Indian luxury hospitality brand’s global expansion. The landmark property becomes the first Taj hotel in mainland Europe, bringing the brand’s signature blend of heritage, service, and luxury to one of Germany’s most important financial and cultural hubs.
Originally one of Frankfurt’s most iconic luxury hotels, the historic Hessischer Hof has undergone extensive restoration before reopening under the Taj banner. The property combines its rich legacy with contemporary luxury, while introducing celebrated Taj experiences including Indian hospitality, refined dining concepts, and the renowned Jimmy’s Bar.
The opening represents another significant step in Taj’s international growth strategy as the brand continues to expand its presence across key gateway cities around the world.

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
There is a quiet power to Rukmini Vasanth (@rukmini_vasanth). One that doesn’t seek attention, yet holds it effortlessly.
For Manifest’s May–June cover, she steps into an imaginative world where temple jewellery meets contemporary silhouettes, blurring the lines between memory and modernity. She embodies a woman in transition, grounded, luminous, and yet constantly becoming.
“I’m not very good at describing myself,” she says, almost in passing.
Yet, in the same breath, her choices reveal a quiet clarity of intent. “I don’t want to isolate my character. I need to understand the whole world it exists in.”
Between restraint and reinvention, stillness and movement, she exists in that rare in-between. Not fully defined, not confined. Just on the cusp of a wider sky.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.
Chairperson: Avarna Jain (@avarna_jain)

posts.caption:
The Mystique of Meenakari
Some crafts quietly recede into history, and others continually reinvent themselves without losing their essence. Meenakari belongs firmly to the latter. Centuries old yet strikingly relevant, this intricate art of enamelling metal carries a timeless allure that feels both historic and refreshingly modern.

posts.caption:
Fibremaxxing is no drama, no detox energy. Just your meals, upgraded quietly.
After years of protein overload and complicated diet rules, fibre is having its quiet comeback and this time it’s not asking for much. It is basically the idea of doing the most practical thing for your body: eating more fibre-rich foods across your day. Think fruits, vegetables, pulses, whole grains, seeds—nothing extreme, just more intentional layering of what already works.
We spoke to Akshita Singla (@akshitasingla), Co-founder of Akya Wellness and a certified nutrition coach, who explains fibremaxxing is less about chasing trends and more about correcting imbalance.
Link in Bio🔗
Written by @mirzashainaa
[Holistic Health, Healthy Skin, Nutrition, Collagen, Hormones, Nourish]

posts.caption:
Men have been expressing themselves through jewellery for some time now—some are already fully leaning in, while others still sit somewhere between curiosity and hesitation.
Let’s be honest, jewellery for men isn’t always about fitting into a trend or dressing around a concept. It’s simply another way to express who you are, much like clothes, fragrance, or even a haircut. The idea that jewellery belongs to a gender feels outdated in a moment where personal style is increasingly fluid, intuitive, and individual.
We’ve pulled together a curated selection of styles and pieces to show how jewellery can slot into your rotation—with a little ease, a little personality, and just the right amount of pizzazz.

posts.caption:
As we approach the Knicks’ first appearance in the NBA finals since 1999, fans with the misfortune of not being named Timothée Chalamet or Spike Lee have been cursing the gut-punching ticket prices for the team’s home games at Madison Square Garden.
But tickets are not the only Knicks commodity to have peaked during the team’s providential playoff run. The market for vintage Knicks apparel has soared, as have the prices. Over the weekend on eBay, a tee from the 1999 finals sold for $350. What is the obsession with vintage merch?
"To wear a tee from the ’90s or the ’00s is to demonstrate (genuinely or not) that you have been with the team through the good times and the bad," our fashion reporter, Jacob Gallagher, wrote. In his latest The Fashions newsletter, @jacobwgallagher talked to vintage clothing sellers in New York about keeping up with the demand. Tap the link in our bio to read the full story and subscribe to The Fashions. Photo by Nathaniel S. Butler/NBAE, via Getty Images

posts.caption:
To celebrate Marilyn Monroe's 100th birthday, 1,037 fans came together to dress as the screen legend in downtown Palm Springs, beneath her giant statue.
In doing so, they set a Guinness World Record in the very place where her career began. Palm Springs — whose identity is inextricably linked with the star — likes to claim Marilyn Monroe, born Norma Jeane Mortensen, and the feeling was mutual.
Participants arrived with Marilyn handbags, Marilyn necklaces, Marilyn earrings, Marilyn umbrellas and enough red lipstick to supply the entire city. Blonde wigs and white dresses were provided by Palm Springs Pride, the organizers of the citywide birthday party. Though all the wigs were the same, no one appeared to be celebrating the same Marilyn Monroe.
At the link in our bio, read about what the cinematic legend means to her fans and see more photos from the record-breaking event. Visuals by @francesca.forquet
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on June 02, 2026. May be an image of ‎basketball jersey, sportswear, crowd and ‎text that says '‎198 4 116 EW YDRK NEWYORKmohS RAGAS Knicks Fans Want '90s Tees. They'll ו1' Have to Pay Very 2026 Prices.‎'‎‎.

posts.caption:
Anyone walking down the street in the ’90s would not have been surprised to see a passerby toggling between a couple of pagers at once, and maybe retrieving an early flip phone or a Discman from a Kate Spade purse.
But after years of progressive slimming down and merging of our electronics into single devices that do everything, clunky seems to be back. The screens are bigger, the headphones are more obtrusive and, crucially, there are two or even three phones on hand.
It may seem counterintuitive, but many people who carry two cellphones believe it’s the healthier choice, allowing for clearer boundaries between work and life. But the poly phone life comes with the inconvenience of having to actually carry them. And everyone handles it a bit differently.
From stacking to pocket stuffing, tap the link in our bio to see how New Yorkers are managing the multiple-phone life. Photos by @instadickie
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on June 02, 2026. May be an image of ‎basketball jersey, sportswear, crowd and ‎text that says '‎198 4 116 EW YDRK NEWYORKmohS RAGAS Knicks Fans Want '90s Tees. They'll ו1' Have to Pay Very 2026 Prices.‎'‎‎.

posts.caption:
Every lifestyle entrepreneur needs a home grand enough to serve as an aspirational backdrop. Martha Stewart had Turkey Hill, her historic farmhouse in Westport, Connecticut. Hannah Neeleman has Ballerina Farm, a 328-acre spread in Kamas, Utah.
Nabela Noor found such a place two years ago, an 11,000-square-foot white-brick Georgian Revival mansion, built in 1912, in York, Pennsylvania, the small city where she grew up. The daughter of immigrants from Bangladesh, Noor has been a full-time digital creator for a decade, and her social media followers, more than 12 million of them, have watched her transform her home and grounds with her family.
Now, the mansion serves as the setting for the new Tubi streaming series “Hosted by Nabela Noor,” as she moves her idealized take on family life beyond the social media platforms where she started. The New York Times got a tour of her home and spoke with @nabela about her content, the show and what's next for her and her family. Tap the link in our bio to read the full article. Photos by @schaunchampion
posts.alt_text: Photo by New York Times Fashion & Style on June 02, 2026. May be an image of ‎basketball jersey, sportswear, crowd and ‎text that says '‎198 4 116 EW YDRK NEWYORKmohS RAGAS Knicks Fans Want '90s Tees. They'll ו1' Have to Pay Very 2026 Prices.‎'‎‎.

posts.caption:
Walking through SoHo with Maurice Kamara, you get the sense that he is judging every outfit he sees. Often, he likes to ask strangers — and, increasingly, celebrities — about their outfits on camera, posting the videos on his Instagram account the People Gallery.
At its core @thepeoplegallery_ is a document of personal style. The account has featured Kim Kardashian, Michael B. Jordan and Anna Wintour. His interviews with celebrities come off as spur of the moment, but in reality, they typically require a decent amount of planning — or waiting. “A lot of people don’t know that it’s a lot of work,” Kamara told The New York Times.
But interspersed among the celebrities are video interviews with everyday people whose outfits simply happened to catch his eye. At the link in our bio, more about Kamara’s passion for fashion and how he has built his account into what it is now. Photos by @aundre

posts.caption:
Rachel Dratch, the veteran comedian beloved for her “Saturday Night Live” characters like "Debbie Downer," is finally getting her due, with her popular, mystical "Woo Woo" podcast and a return to the Broadway stage.
In late March, Dratch began her run as the Narrator in “The Rocky Horror Show” at Studio 54, a role that has earned her her second Tony nomination. (Four years ago, she was nominated for her role as Stephanie in “POTUS: Or, Behind Every Great Dumbass Are Seven Women Trying to Keep Him Alive.”)
The role of the Narrator is often performed by an older white man. But Sam Pinkleton, the show's director, envisioned Dratch in the role for a reason: “Nobody can do dry, understated, commanding humor quite like Rachel,” he said.
After she left “S.N.L.” in 2006, Dratch's career seemed to be slowing down, and she started to panic about her future. “And then I sort of decided not to,” she told The New York Times. Read more about how @raedratch not only manifested her dreams, but is exceeding them, at the link in our bio. Photo by @ok__mccausland

posts.caption:
Once again @samvissermakeup and his team led the way for a dramatic return to the early-2000s “party-girl” glamour for #Demna’s first #Gucci cruise show last night in New York. The signature heavy smudged smoky shadow often wrapped around the entire eye and blown out along the lower lash line. A subtle nod to the #TomFord and #FridaGiannini era.
images courtesy of @gucci @myfacewheno_o
posts.alt_text: Photo by umar on May 15, 2026. May be an image of espadrilles, sandals, high-heeled shoes, slingback shoes, toecap shoes and text that says 'Dior Tieat Christian Riger lever Feger ChritianDior hriitian Dior UN Is This Fashion's Most Influential Shoe Designer?'.

posts.caption:
#SadieSink’s custom @prada look styled by @mollyddickson got me thinking - is the Regency fashion era back? Emerging in the late 18th and early 19th century, the empire silhouette was inspired by Greco-Roman sculpture and the cultural changes following the French Revolution. Marking a departure from structured court dress, replacing heavy corsetry and panniers.
The Regency revival can partly be influenced by the rise of #Bridgerton, repositioning the empire silhouette as a modern look. Recently, both Emma Stone and #Zendaya wore @louisvuitton looks reminiscent of Regency wear.
This influential era has always been part of fashion, with designers like #JohnGalliano romantically referencing the empire line waist during his tenure at Givenchy and Dior. #JonathanAnderson’s Dior tailoring, waistcoats, high collars, and elongated coats, is also reminiscent of early nineteenth-century dandy dress.
Images courtesy of Getty / Netflix / Givenchy / Prada
posts.alt_text: Photo by umar on May 15, 2026. May be an image of espadrilles, sandals, high-heeled shoes, slingback shoes, toecap shoes and text that says 'Dior Tieat Christian Riger lever Feger ChritianDior hriitian Dior UN Is This Fashion's Most Influential Shoe Designer?'.

posts.caption:
Last week, #Dior presented their latest nature-inspired fine jewelry collection by Victoire de Castellane in Venice. Alongside the jewels, I couldn’t help but notice #JonathanAnderson’s unofficial second “couture collection”.
Jonathan created 20 #HauteCouture looks to accompany the jewels, and while his actual second couture collection for Dior is in July, this presentation gave us another glimpse at his work. Letting the jewels take centre stage, the looks seemed to be much more refined, with plenty of nods to his signature draping and silhouettes from Cruise, while steeped in #ChristianDior’s botanical romanticism.
What do you think of this couture collection compared to his debut?
Images courtesy of Anaick Lejart & Dior
posts.alt_text: Photo by umar on May 15, 2026. May be an image of espadrilles, sandals, high-heeled shoes, slingback shoes, toecap shoes and text that says 'Dior Tieat Christian Riger lever Feger ChritianDior hriitian Dior UN Is This Fashion's Most Influential Shoe Designer?'.

posts.caption:
#DonatellaVersace’s shows in the 2000s were undeniably iconic, she understood music and fashion worked hand in hand. Her shows felt like stepping into a nightclub. Models stomped, took sharp turns and held eye contact, amplifying the sense of excess and spectacle that defined the era.
My all time favourite was her Fall 2006 runway which featured a mashup of artists like #MichaelJackson, #Madonna, M.I.A., and #Blondie. It Captured decades of pop culture and fashion - exactly what #Versace was, where fashion, celebrity, music, and luxury was inseparable and excessive.

posts.caption:
One of #JohnGalliano’s most iconic pieces for #Dior was the Masai pearl choker. First shown in his debut 1997 #HauteCouture collection, he was inspired from the elongated neck adornments associated with Maasai beadwork and stacked collars from East Africa. Rather than sitting on the collarbone, the choker frames the entire neck, forcing the wearer into an upright posture kind of like a corset, but for the neck.
Throughout his 15 year tenure, the necklace was reimagined in multiple forms and materials, becoming a recognisable signature of his era. Today, the chokers are highly sought after, selling for an average of £10,000 on the resale market.  A symbol of Galliano’s maximalist vision for #ChristianDior #jewelry

posts.caption:
The Madras High Court has upheld the divorce granted to a retired Army man and held that repeated allegations of extramarital affairs made by his estranged wife to his military superiors, and removing ‘Thali’ or mangalsutra, besides the nearly three-decade separation period constituted mental cruelty under the Hindu Marriage Act.
The court said, "It is known fact that no Hindu married woman would remove the thali at any point of time during the lifetime of her husband. Thali around the neck of a wife is a sacred thing which symbolises the continuance of married life and it is removed only after the death of Husband. Therefore, the removal of thali by the petitioner/wife can be said to be an act, which reflected Mental Cruelty of highest order as it could have caused agony and hurted the sentiments of the respondent."
The couple had gotten married in 1977. According to the husband, the wife frequently quarrelled with him saying that he had illegal contact with several women and insulted him in public. He also alleged that the wife had even sent letters to his superior officers in the Army and lodged a police complaint against him, for which he was convicted for 7 years which was later reduced on appeal.
Source: Live Law
Visit www.shethepeople.tv for more.
#india #trending #news  marriage indian wedding South indian marriage divorce Madras HC

posts.caption:
#Opinion | Marriage has always been a very big deal in India, but have you ever noticed the pre-wedding preparations for women? From weight loss drugs to intrusive skincare treatments, bridal beauty packages are booming even more these days, raising important questions about evolving beauty standards
In India, it’s very common to see girls taught to focus on their appearance right from childhood. "Don't go out to play or you'll get tanned." "Don't eat that or you'll put on weight." The same upbringing reflects during marriage. How many men have we seen going to extreme lengths to achieve the perfect "groom" look? Barely any, right?
Beauty standards continuously push women towards extremely harmful treatments for that perfection. What once started with homemade beauty DIYs has now extended to injections and chemicals.
From ‘crash dieting’, where one skips meals and eats extremely low calories, to GLP-1 drugs like Mounjaro, Wegovy, or Ozempic, the pursuit of weight loss has shifted dramatically. The weight loss drug market is forecast to reach 80 billion rupees ($851.79 million) by 2030, according to Reuters.
Read full article by Ankita Kundu @_._i_am_ankita30_._ at www.shethepeople.tv
#bride #wedding bridal glow, bridal lehenga, bridal mehendi, bridal look, indian bride, north indian bride, south indian bride, Maharashtrian bride, Bengali bride, skincare, weight loss, pre wedding shoot

posts.caption:
A Bumble match that ended up at Villa Balbiano.
Sometimes the story writes itself - and then it sets itself on the shores of Lake Como, dresses itself in Tarun Tahiliani, lets the rain kiss the mandap during the ceremony, and waits for the sun to break through right after the varmala. As if it was always going to.
That is Kinji and Herit’s love story.
And then there was them. ❤️
But what made this celebration impossible to look away from was how every element arrived at exactly the right moment - the bold blooms holding their own against the grandeur of the villa, the heirlooms carrying the weight of where they came from, and two people standing on the edge of Lake Como looking like they had always belonged there.
Some love stories begin in unexpected places.
And some stay with you because of how completely they came full circle - from a swipe to vows on one of the most beautiful lakes in the world, with the rain and the sun and the whole of Como bearing witness. 🌟

Not every bridal look is just an outfit. Some are a whole identity - woven from the soil, the stories, and the centuries of beauty that came before. 🌸
Think of your bridal aesthetic not just as what you wear on the day… but as the version of yourself you’ve always known you’d become the moment you walked into that mandap.
The Rajasthani bride who wears her heritage like armour - all kundan and crimson, regal before she even enters the room. The Maharashtrian bride in her Paithani, where every zari thread holds a generation of grace. The Bengali bride whose shola-mukut and alta-painted feet make her look like she stepped out of a painting that was waiting to be finished. 💫
The Punjabi bride blazing in red and gold, carrying the weight of every celebration her family has ever thrown. The Kerala bride whose ivory kasavu and temple jewellery make quiet the most powerful thing in the room. And the one who chooses royalcore - because she always knew she was built for gold.
It’s never just about the state you’re from… It’s about the aesthetic that was always yours - the one that makes your mother tear up before the baraat even arrives.
Because India doesn’t have one kind of beautiful. It has hundreds.
Which bridal aesthetic speaks to you? Drop your state below. 🤍

A Hindu wedding ceremony that knew exactly what it was.
Rooted in tradition, held together by emotion, and then - just when the mood had settled into something beautifully solemn - a bike pulled up and Coe arrived like the universe had decided the day needed one more unforgettable moment.
It did.
And then there was them. ❤️
But what made Reevah and Coe’s celebration linger long after the last frame was how completely it held both things at once - the depth of the rituals grounding the day in something real, and the personality of the couple refusing to be contained by convention. A Bollywood hero energy that didn’t clash with the ceremony. It completed it.
Some weddings are traditional.
And some stay with you because they were traditional and entirely themselves - the pheras felt, the emotions unguarded, and a groom’s entry that shifted the mood of the whole room in the best possible way. 🌟

posts.caption:
Not every groom look is just about how sharp it is. Some carry confidence in the way they’re worn… quietly, yet with an unmistakable presence. ✨
Minimal groom styling is one of those statements.
Beyond the heavy embroidery, the maximalist layering, and the louder choices… there’s a kind of stillness here.
A groom who lets the moment speak, rather than competing with it.
And as weddings evolve, so does what grooms reach for… no longer just traditional heavily-embellished sherwanis, but thoughtfully curated looks that reflect restraint, taste, and the kind of confidence that needs no decoration.
From clean ivory bandis that feel almost sculptural, to muted pastels that carry an effortless calm, to crisp whites and soft neutrals that hold their own against any backdrop… each look brings its own quiet authority to the mandap.
Some are stripped back, letting posture and presence do the talking. Others are minimal yet textured… a subtle weave, a clean silhouette, a single well-chosen detail that elevates without overwhelming.
Think of minimal groom styling as more than just a trend… it’s a perspective. The understanding that elegance rarely needs to announce itself.
Because long after the photographs have been framed… it’s the looks that felt effortless that the camera - and everyone in the room - couldn’t look away from. 💫
Which minimal look would you make yours? ✨

posts.caption:
Fashion styling is a job. Not a hobby. Not a passion project. Not something people do on the side because they love clothes.
It is a profession that takes years to build. The eye. The contacts. The knowledge of how the industry actually works across every sector, every brief, every type of client. That does not come from loving fashion. It comes from years of showing up, doing the work and figuring it out mostly alone.
And yet it is still one of the most undervalued roles in the creative industry.
Stylists are asked to work for free. For credit. For exposure. For a rate that does not come close to reflecting the skill, the hours or the experience involved. And somewhere along the way the industry decided that was normal. That stylists should be grateful for the opportunity. That loving what you do means you should not expect to be paid properly for it.
We are past that.
Behind every iconic image, every red carpet moment, every campaign that stopped you mid-scroll, there was a stylist. Building the looks, interpreting the brief, managing the pressure, absorbing the chaos so nobody else had to. Often without a credit. Sometimes without a proper rate.
That changes when stylists know their worth, charge it without apology and stop accepting the jobs that treat their career like a favour.
➡️➡️ If you are a working fashion stylist who is ready to do exactly that, comment STUDIO and we will send you the free trial link to The Studio of Stylist Elixir. Rate guides. Contracts. Portfolio tools. Masterclasses every Monday. Everything you need to build a career that reflects what you are actually worth.
Save this. Send it to a stylist who needs to hear it.

posts.caption:
Be honest. How many times have you had to explain that your job is not just “picking out nice clothes.” 🙄
You smile and nod at the dinner party while someone tells you they could do what you do. Meanwhile you are the one who got the call at 11pm because the samples did not clear customs. 📦 You are the one who held the whole shoot together when the brief changed on the day. You are the one who went home, lay awake doing sums, and wondered why a job this demanding still feels this uncertain. 😮‍💨
That is the part nobody at the dinner party sees.
They think it is a glamorous day out with beautiful clothes and a coffee. ☕️ They do not see the fifty pulls from twelve showrooms. The personalities you manage on every set. The private client who needs handling with care, the celebrity look that has to be signed off in days, the commercial brief with notes at every stage. They do not see you building a look out of nothing while a whole crew waits on you, then keeping going through the quiet months when the diary goes still and you start to doubt yourself. 🥀
You are not bad at this. You are doing one of the hardest creative jobs there is, mostly on your own, with no one in your corner who actually gets it. 💔
That is the bit I want to change. ✨
✦ The Studio of Stylist Elixir is a paid membership community for fashion stylists who are done figuring it all out alone. ✦

posts.caption:
Ask any stylist five years in what they wish they had known earlier, and the answers are almost always the same.
Your rate is a decision, not a fixed price. The business side is the actual career, not the chore. Always use a contract, every job, no exceptions. Saying no is how you grow.
Relationships outlast every single job. You do not have to learn it all alone. And the career rewards the ones who build on purpose, not the ones who wait to get lucky.
The lessons are not complicated. The expensive part is the years it takes to learn them
the hard way.
✦ The earlier you start building on purpose, the further you go. ✦
💚 For the stylist in their first five years right now💚 For anyone tired of learning it all
the hard way💚
For every working stylist ready to build on purpose
▸ DM  MENTOR to find out if the Fashion Mentorship Program is right for you. Doors close 1 June.
PS. We broke down the ten things every styling contract needs over on our Substack.

posts.caption:
The most talented stylist in the room is not always the one with the biggest career. That has nothing to do with ability.
Talent gets you in. It is not what levels you up. The business side is where most careers break, the rates, the pitching, the contracts, the part talent alone cannot carry.
Undercharging becomes a habit. There is never a plan beyond the next booking. And doing it all alone is not noble, it is just slow.
Stuck is not a verdict on your talent. It is a stage, and stages can be moved through.
✦ Fix what is missing around the talent, and the talent that was always there finally gets to move. ✦
💚 For the talented stylist whose career will not move💚 For anyone tired of getting it wrong first to learn it
💚 For every stylist who knows the ability was never the issue

posts.caption:
You already know you are ready. That is the hard part, and you are past it.
You are turning down work you would have begged for two years ago. Your rate has not moved in three seasons while your work has got better every single one. You are the most experienced person on set and still being booked, and billed, like an assistant. The diary is full. The bank balance is not. And you are quietly exhausted from figuring all of it out completely alone.
Here is the truth nobody says out loud. You will not grow out of this on your own. Not because you are not good enough, you clearly are, but because talent was never the thing holding you back. The business was. The plan was. The room of people who have already done it was.
✦ Ready is not the finish line. It is the moment right before everything changes. ✦
So picture six months from now. You quote your new rate without your voice shaking, and they say yes. Your diary is full of the work you actually want, the work that builds the name you are after. You stop guessing at your next move because you finally have a plan you believe in. That is not a fantasy. That is six weeks of work away.
The Fashion Mentorship Program is how you get there. A small group, six weeks, your portfolio, your rates, your positioning and your plan rebuilt from the ground up, with direct support the whole way through.
It starts 1st August, and it is the last mentorship program of 2026. After this group, the next chance is a year away. Doors close Saturday 30th May. That is days, not weeks.
If you are ready, do not let this be another thing you tell yourself you will do next time. There is no next time this year.
💚 For the stylist who has outgrown the level they are stuck on

posts.caption:
This is not India’s first Paris couture moment. It is something more specific: one of India’s most visible couture businesses entering one of fashion’s most selective calendars.
Manish Malhotra (@manishmalhotra05), who began in Hindi cinema costume design in 1990, will present on 8 July at Paris Haute Couture Week. Rahul Mishra and Gaurav Gupta have already shifted the idea of what Indian couture can look like in Paris.
For decades, India’s role in the global luxury system has often been positioned behind the scenes: embroidery ateliers, textile production and specialised craft work executed for European fashion houses rather than presented under Indian labels.
Malhotra brings a different vocabulary: spectacle, celebrity, bridal fantasy and red-carpet scale.
Paris couture has often preferred Indian fashion when it arrives with some degree of translation. Malhotra’s debut adds another perspective to that conversation, bringing a different expression of Indian couture to the calendar.

posts.caption:
India’s classrooms are entering the AI age at massive scale, with 25 crore students and 15 lakh schools across the country.
The promise is curiosity, even though access remains uneven. As a result, classroom reality can look more like copy-paste sameness. CBSE’s AI curriculum is set to roll out from Class 3 in the 2026-27 academic year, pitched as a “universal skill”. On ground, teachers are still dealing with weak guardrails, limited training, rote-learning habits, and students turning to ChatGPT for answers before they learn how to ask better questions.
Platforms like SATHEE promise access and personalised learning. But without stronger systems, AI risks scaling the problems Indian education has been trying to solve for decades. What is unfolding is a cultural reset in classrooms, where the future of learning depends on whether AI expands curiosity or flattens thought.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Arshia Dhar (@urseaa)
#AIineducation #IndianSchools #CopyPasteCulture #LearningWithoutCuriosity #FutureOfPedagogy

posts.caption:
A viral song today arrives already broken into parts: the hook for recall, the lyric for captions, the beat for choreography, the mood for someone else’s edit.
In India, musicians are now writing for the song and the scroll, where a few seconds can push a track harder than an old-school rollout.
The shift has made discovery more democratic and more chaotic. Forgotten tracks return through memes and wedding videos. Official audio can turn clips into royalties.
A song can travel faster than any label budget and still risk becoming just another piece of content moving at algorithm speed.
Read more at the link in bio.
By Karishma Kuenzang (@kkuenzang)
#viralsong #instagramviralsong #viralhits #musicdistribution #culture

posts.caption:
The male body, once allowed a certain laziness, is now expected to show its working. At some point, getting hotter stopped sounding fun.
Looksmaxxing sits at that extreme end. It is male vanity after it has been given a scoring system. In a culture where status, masculinity and identity can feel unstable, looksmaxxing offers the illusion of control.
The manosphere gives that promise its sharper ideological edge, folding self-improvement into fear of rejection, romantic inadequacy, peer comparison and the belief that appearance determines social worth.
Terms like “low-tier man”, “subhuman” and “Giga Chad” reduce human identity into rankings and visual metrics.
“Bone-smashing is dangerous and has no scientific basis whatsoever. Mewing is wildly overhyped; there is no robust clinical evidence it meaningfully reshapes adult craniofacial structure,” says Dr Macrene Alexiades.
Head to the link in bio to read why the future of looksmaxxing may not be its most brutal version, but its most ordinary-looking one.
By Ria Bhatia (@riabbhatia)

Leading the launch are the highly anticipated Pocket Bronze and Highlight Milk, alongside new shades of Peptide Lip Tint and Peptide Lip Shape.
The goal? Sun-kissed skin, glossy bronze lips, and that effortless “I just got back from vacation” glow without actually having to leave your inbox behind.

posts.caption:
Dua Lipa is officially married!!! According to reports (and photos probably already circulating your feed), she and Callum Turner tied the knot in an intimate civil ceremony at Old Marylebone Town Hall, surrounded by a small group of family and friends.
Naturally, the fashion was impeccable. Dua reportedly wore a custom ivory Schiaparelli haute couture skirt suit styled with a dramatic Stephen Jones hat, white gloves, and Christian Louboutin pumps, while Turner opted for classic navy tailoring.
And if you thought that was it, think again. Reports suggest the newlyweds are planning a larger multi-day celebration in Sicily next week, with Villa Valguarnera heavily rumored as the setting.
Congratulations to the happy couple. I’m not jealous at all…
#DuaLipa #CallumTurner #WeddingStyle #Schiaparelli #CelebrityNews

posts.caption:
Safe to say it’s finally summer, and there was a lot you may have missed this past week in fashion. Between Heaven Mayhem’s refreshed seasonal branding, Chanel pulling out all the stops with Jacob Elordi as the face of the new Bleu de Chanel campaign, and Celine continuing to fuel everyone’s seaside daydreams through its latest campaign imagery, the fashion people are being fed right now.
A lot of the industry feels like it’s leaning back into fantasy this season: sun-drenched escapism, cinematic storytelling…
No complaints from me.
#FashionNews #SummerFashion #Chanel #JacobElordi #Celine

posts.caption:
Less than six weeks out from Paris Haute Couture Week, and it’s officially crunch time. Inside the ateliers, it’s all hands: hundreds of hours going into hand-sewing, 3D embellishment, beadwork, and the kind of techniques that don’t shortcut—ribbon weaving, feather work, lace appliqué. Every look is being finished, refined, and reworked until it’s exact.
At the same time, designers are locking in show flow of sequencing looks, mapping pacing, timing changes down to the second. And behind it all, PR is in full operation mode. Translating months of craftsmanship into global visibility, press moments, and cultural impact.
Six weeks. High pressure. Zero margin.
#HauteCouture #ParisCouture #FashionWeek #LuxuryFashion #TheCitizensPoste

posts.caption:
@_pattaraphan_ is one of those brands that makes you pause mid-scroll. Founded by Pattaraphan “Nok” Salirathavibhaga, the Bangkok-based label sits right at the intersection of sculpture and fine jewelry—clean, minimal, but with just enough edge to feel personal. Every piece is made locally in Bangkok, grounded in Thai craftsmanship but designed with a global, contemporary eye.
Launched in 2018, the brand was built to fill a gap: fine jewelry that didn’t feel traditional or overly polished, but still carried weight. Genderless, intentional, and quietly distinctive.
And clearly, people are paying attention. From FKA Twigs to Bella Hadid, it’s become one of those insider brands that signals taste without trying too hard.
Understated, but not forgettable.
#Pattaraphan #FineJewelry #JewelryDesign #Minimalism #Bangkok

posts.caption:
Major fashion houses cycle through creative directors for a few core reasons: shifting aesthetics, evolving philosophies, financial pressure, and the constant churn of consumer demand. At a brand like Gucci, that tension has turned the role of creative director into something much bigger than a traditional couturier.
We’ve seen this pattern before in different forms across the industry: moments of extreme cultural saturation that feel successful in real time, but become harder to defend once the cycle moves on. Attention alone doesn’t rebuild a business model, and it certainly doesn’t guarantee loyalty—either from long-standing Gucci customers or from the newer audience drawn in by spectacle.
Read the latest on Substack: The Citizen’s Poste | posteposteposte.com
#Gucci #Demna #LaFamiglia #FashionCritic #TomFord

Lagoon blue, sunset orange, transparent silver - each one feels easy, a bit playful in a way that just works!
It’s a familiar silhouette done in a new way, made for warm days, late nights, and not taking things too seriously.
And if you’re wondering how to wear them, Barbara Palvin already has it figured out.
#TFO #fashion #JimmyChoo #JellyShoe

posts.caption:
do you remember Ungaro?
founded by Emanuel Ungaro in 1965, the Parisian fashion house was once one of the biggest names in luxury fashion
in the ’80s and ’90s, wearing Ungaro meant making a statement. the brand dressed celebrities, royalty and some of the most influential women of the era, becoming synonymous with unapologetic glamour
yet despite its enormous success, Ungaro gradually faded from the spotlight as fashion trends shifted and new luxury giants took centre stage.
the funny thing? looking at today’s runways, with their love of maximalism, colour and statement dressing, Emanuel Ungaro’s influence feels more relevant than ever
fashion may move on, but great style never really disappears
#TFO #vintage #emanuelungaro

posts.caption:
For Adarsh Gourav (@gouravadarsh)—the BAFTA-nominated actor who had the dream debut of playing a younger Shah Rukh Khan in ‘My Name is Khan’ (2010) and has, in the last 15 years, worked with bucket-list filmmakers like Zoya Akhtar, Bejoy Nambiar, Ramin Bahrani, and Arjun Varain Singh—music has always been his first love. It’s a love seeded in his childhood, one that has stuck despite often being unrequited, through rejection, failure, and harsh realisations. It has been his Everest, and it has given him everything he has today; he would likely not be on screen if it weren’t for being “noticed” while singing ‘Vande Mataram’ at the Kala Ghoda Arts Festival as a teenager, which really put him on the path to auditioning for films.
“I’ve always wanted to make music. I wanted to be a playback singer,” he says now, relaying a parallel life story that’s stayed under wraps until very recently. “And now I’m just using my street cred as an actor to push this side of myself. And why not? I hope they’ll think: ‘Acting achhi kar leta hai toh ye bhi theek thaak kar hi leta hoga.’”
At the link in bio, @_niddee_ chats with the polymath about the rare vantage of enjoying the best of both worlds.

posts.alt_text:
Growing up in Chennai as the youngest of three siblings, acting wasn’t part of the plan for model student Samantha Ruth Prabhu (@samantharuthprabhuoffl). During her gap year after college, the then 21-year-old dabbled in modelling and ended up at a film audition for kicks. “I thought if I do get it, I’ll do it for a year and then go back to studying,” she says. “The idea was to do it temporarily, never make it a career. I’m sure my teachers were disappointed when I became an actor. I think some of them still are,” she laughs.
  
We’re not the first to ask her what her alternative career would be, but this time she has an answer: “I’m doing it now,” she says with excitement. “I think this entrepreneurial journey is what I’ve always wanted to do,” says the serial entrepreneur, whose work straddles films, beauty, wellness, fashion, food, and sport, all at once.

For our second anniversary issue, @chloechou13 flew to Hyderabad to chat with our cover star. Full interview at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Remember when vacations were treated like major life events? You spent months planning them, saved up all year, and returned with enough photographs to make everyone sit through a two-hour slideshow. The annual holiday wasn’t just any trip. It was the trip.
For many millennials and the generations before them, that model made sense. School calendars revolved around long summer vacations, and families would pile into cars or board trains for a two-week trip that had been discussed for months beforehand. Gen Z, inventors of micro retirement and many other micro trends, are adding mini breaks to their widely expanding legacy.
According to Never The Same, a new YouGov survey conducted on behalf of Airbnb (@airbnb)among more than 2,000 young Indians across 11 cities, 7 in 10 Gen Z travellers said they would rather take three short trips than one long annual holiday.
At the link in bio, @sheyakurian on the survey that revealed how Gen Z likes to travel.
Illustrations: @diyapaulart

posts.caption:
The Indian fashion retail landscape might be crowded, and while there’s no shortage of bridalwear boutiques and Instagram-famous labels, there’s space for a store that brings together south-east Asian designers from across the Subcontinent in a way that feels considered.
At AltaRasa (@thealtarasa), hand-painted batik pieces by Sri Lankan designer Sonali Dharmawardena sit next to joyful, Bella Hadid-approved knitwear by Palestinian-origin designer Reema Al Banna of Reemami. There’s sculptural occasion wear by Indonesian designer Stella Rissa alongside established Indian labels such as Akaaro, Re-ceremonial, and Kiran Uttam Ghosh gracing the racks, while brands such as Idli by Thierry Journo—a Jaipur circuit favourite—can now be accessed by more consumers.
“We wanted to juxtapose the known with the quieter brands doing incredible work,” Lopamudra explains of the labels you’ll find here, many of whom are debuting in India with AltaRasa.
At the link in bio, @tanya.91 on the multi-designer store curated with intention.

posts.caption:
In certain circles in London—specifically, the group chats of women losing their hair and mind during perimenopause—the name Dr Archana Rao comes up a lot. She runs one of the very few NHS clinics in the UK dedicated entirely to skin and hair. The waiting list is a year. Her research has been published in Nature Communications. She lectures doctors and trichologists across the globe. This month, the dermatologist is opening a second private clinic in Wimbledon because even the year-long waitlist, it turns out, isn’t meeting demand.
Her approach is unglamorous and, in the age of viral serums and supplement stacks, radical in its insistence on first principles. There’s a questionnaire to fill before the appointment. Then a full blood panel: not just iron but ferritin, thyroid panel, B12, folate, vitamin D, zinc, liver, and renal profiles. A proper look at the scalp. And then the referrals: a psychologist for the woman who is clearly not fine but insisting she is; a dietician for the patient whose iron stores are so depleted no topical treatment will touch it; an endocrinologist for the hormonal complexity that falls outside her lane.
At the link in bio, @shereegg30 on the dermatologist changing the game for peri-menopausal and menopausal woman.

posts.caption:
Do you fancy yourself as Lady Bridgerton? Carrying illicit notes for your lover in a tiny coin purse whose click perhaps gave away your secret to the entire ton? If you think of yourself as a modern fire-starter, you may be a little partial to the new legion of kiss-lock bags that have taken over luxury store fronts and the high street.
What is a kiss-lock bag, you ask? It’s a bag or pouch with a metal frame and a vintage clasp closure, where two metal knobs “kiss” shut with a satisfying click.
Millennials will confess to the joy of snapping shut a flip phone, the prim satisfaction of banging the door on someone who was not welcome, or the satisfying crack of a ball hitting the sweet spot on the tennis racket. The ‘click’ of the kiss-lock closure offers that same smug satisfaction of a job finished…without the actual damage.
At the link in bio, @vinitamakhija on the return of the kiss-lock, and a few you might want to get your hands on.

posts.caption:
Beyond one tragic incident lie deeper structural failures.
Barring a few exceptions, fashion modelling in India continues as an informal profession. Models are expected to show up, perform, travel, pose in difficult locations, and assume risk—often without basic protections that most other industries consider standard. Divyanshu Joshi’s death last week in an Ernakulam quarry while shooting for Kartik Research, is yet another wake-up call for fashion brands, production agencies, casting directors, managements and shoot crews. Also for employers. Especially as Joshi was not just a model, but an employee at Kartik Research.
Here is a minimum code of care list.
✅ Risk assessments before official assignments
✅ Safety protocols at outdoor and hazardous locations
✅ Written contracts and clear liability clauses
✅ Medical and accident insurance
✅ Emergency response procedures
✅ Protection against discrimination, harassment and stereotyping based on gender or sexuality
✅ Timely payments
✅ Mental health safeguards
✅ Compensation and acknowledgment in the event of injury or death
Fashion speaks about image and visibility. But what about responsibility? Read the full piece via link in bio.
🖋️ @shefatwork
#thevofashion

posts.caption:
Hi, I’m Veronica 👋🏼 a sewing pattern designer based in Melbourne, Australia.
I create patterns, tools and resources that help you sew with understanding.
My work focuses on how fabrics behave, how garments are constructed, and how sewing connects us to the history of what we wear.
Sewing Patterns
My patterns are designed for versatility, wearability and fun. They include the Hazel, Freya, Odette, Audrey, Hera, Cordelia and the Sleeve Studio — each combining comfort with thoughtful design details. You’ll also find The Noelle, a free beginner-friendly pattern that introduces my approach to fit and construction.
The Paper Doll
A print-at-home paper doll designed to help you visualise and play with my sewing patterns. You can cut it out and colour it yourself, or use the pre-filled versions featuring my fabric prints and colour palettes. The set includes curvier model options and three skin tones to choose from.
The Conscious Consumer eBook
A practical guide to fabrics, sustainability and sewing with intention. It helps you understand fibre origins, weave structures and how to make long-lasting choices when you sew or buy clothes.
Digital Sewing Tools
Two paid calculators designed for precision:
• Shirring Calculator – works out fabric shrinkage and elastic thread length.
• GSM Calculator – estimates fabric weight and drape.
Available individually or as a discounted bundle.
My PDF sewing patterns are stocked digitally through @fabricgodmother , @spotlightstores , @thefoldline , @joans.finefabric and @thedrapersdaughter .
Fabric and Accessories
You can shop my fabric designs on @spoonflower and find my illustrated phone cases at @thedairy .
Everything I create is designed to help you sew with confidence, understanding and creativity.
For all enquires email us at hello@veronicatuckerthelabel.com

posts.caption:
The algorithm thinks we want to be a TradWife but it’s totally misreading us 👀
I’ve spent all week writing about why millennial women are pausing on sourdough content, what the algorithm does with that pause, and what we actually want which is not what we’re being sold.
This essay covers haptic data, religious pipelines, the GirlBoss era’s hangover, gentrification in human form, and why I went from TradWife curious to something else entirely.
Full essay linked in bio. Free to read.
Now tell me, when you see TradWife content, what’s your gut reaction?
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption:
The books that are building The Goddess Project.
Six months of research into the history of draped cloth as a political act and these are the books sitting on my desk, on my @kobobooks Libra Colour, and in my @thequirkycupcollective reading journal while I do it.
From Elizabeth Wayland Barber reconstructing what women were actually doing in the ancient world, to Victoria Bateman exposing the inaccuracies in how that history has been recorded, to Emily Wilson (the first woman to ever translate the Odyssey into English) and Stephen Fry making Homer genuinely funny.
Every one of these books is informing a carousel, an essay, or a podcast episode in the Goddess Project series. If you want to read alongside me, this is the list.
The Goddess Project carousels are live on this page. The Substack essays go deeper 👉🏼 link in bio.
Watch the full video breakdown of this list and more books on my YouTube channel.
Paid partnership with @thequirkycupcollective 🫶🏼 Gifted by @kobobooks_au #ad
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption:
This is so important for the history of human kind, especially women ❤️
Don’t forget the Gaulish as well. They’re often harder to find than Irish or Welsh mythology because much of it was not written down by the Gauls themselves. When the Romans conquered Gaul, a lot of the oral traditions were lost or blended with Roman religion. It survived mostly through inscriptions, archaeology, and Roman accounts rather than through medieval mythological texts like those preserved in Ireland.
Epona, perhaps the most famous Gaulish goddess. Rosmerta, Nantosuelta, Sirona, Sequena (who I love, goddess of the river La Seine in Paris) reflecting a common Celtic belief that rivers, springs, lakes and landscapes possessed divine feminine spirits.
The Matres or Matronae,they are some of the most fascinating feminine figures in continental Celtic religion. Rather than a single goddess, they seem to embody the sacred feminine as a collective force.
This “triple goddess” motif later appears in many Celtic traditions.❤️❤️❤️
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption: Lovely sleeve. I read left to right as is our custom, lol. I prefer the left. It is elevated, whereas the right is flippant and doesn't honor your work - it's more like saying look at my clever funny impression of this work. Also, putting randomly oriented emojis near your photo is not good, because they are cartoonish and draw the eyes away from your beautiful photo. The only thing I didn't like on the left is the description of the shirring as starting at the elbow when it really starts about mid-bicep. I think sewists want the most accurate description. Beautiful work - the dress is wonderful!
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption:
They spin the thread. They measure it. They cut it.
Three women have governed every life that has ever been lived and held it in their hands. Not even the King of the Gods could change what they had decided ⚡️
It was always women holding the thread.
The Three Fates is part of the Goddess Project - a series tracing the history of women, cloth, and power across every culture that has ever existed.
New to the series? Start with The First Makers: Ancient Mediterranean & Near East which is also available as a podcast episode on The Culture of Cloth 🎧
posts.alt_text: Photo by Veronica Tucker the Label on October 23, 2025.

posts.caption:
“I don’t want to understand everyone; I want a world where I live and let others live. In New York City, I was staying in the middle of a huge building, surrounded by people of different races and religions, and there was no tension whatsoever. Sloterdijk even talks about how we need to learn a certain kind of discretion. That’s what bothers me — the idea that I have to understand your world. How am I supposed to understand your world when neither you understand it nor I understand my own? I’m very superficial in that regard. You have a neighbor — establish some kind of order, with basic respect, and don’t push yourself onto them, nor let them push themselves onto you. I take great pleasure in discretion. Maybe that comes with age.”
For the May Issue of Vogue Adria, we spoke with @SlavojZizeks, one of today’s most renowned philosophers, about the future of the left, the dangers facing modern society, one night stands, and revolution.

posts.caption:
Pleasure Principle is a new series of texts by Vogue Adria dedicated to curious and informed conversations about pleasure. A safe space where we talk about the politics of pleasure through essays and interviews, we discuss how pleasure is shaped, experienced, expressed through bodies, culture, power, identity, psychology. We speak with pleasure scholars, activists, erotic professionals, intimacy coordinators, scientists, psychologists, writers, artists, directors — and write from within it, through philosophy, activism, somatics, intimacy, aesthetics, popular culture and psychology of pleasure.
Pleasure is not a side note. It’s the subject. So, let’s talk about it properly. Read more, link in bio.

posts.caption:
At times, when I hear someone say, “Oh wow, you’re from Mumbai,” I briefly remember that some people yearn for my life, but the feeling never lasts long. A place that had once seemed so large and full of possibilities when I was a child is now just my hometown; just another regular part of the world. But playing tourist for a friend changed something for me. Read more at the link in bio.
Words by: Asma Siddiqui (@ayesemay)

posts.caption:
The women’s world number one @ArynaSabalenka understands that tennis can be opera: full of triumph and despair, grace and slapstick, sin and atonement. The 28-year-old has been at the top of her sport for the last year and a half, with a deep bench of brand associations—including with Gucci, Nike, and Audemars Piguet—to show for it. But since her earliest days on the tour, she’s been a fiery on-court presence, known for her outbursts (she’s a world-class racket-smasher) and tense exchanges with officials. (She attributes much of this to being “very much a Taurus”).
Time and training have helped her to temper that fire—but it also has its uses. “This is part of my personality that can drive me crazy, but it can also drive me into that real fight mode and help me play with passion,” Sabalenka says. “It’s two sides of the medal.”
For Vogue’s May 2026 digital cover, @robertjhaskell meets Aryna Sabalenka in Rome to talk winning, losing, grieving, learning, and never letting up.
Photographed by @stevenkleinstudio,Written by @robertjhaskell, Styled by @tonnegood, Hair by @sandyhullett, Makeup by @markcarrasquillo, Manicure by @nailsbyetsuko, Tailoring by @sara.on.set.tailor, Produced by @thatoneproduction, Shot on Location at @faenamiamibeach.  Vogue, May digital cover 2026.

posts.caption: Summer has officially kicked off in New York, and with it comes the beginning of the 2026 CFDA/Vogue Fashion Fund. This year’s 10 finalists, selected yesterday at CFDA headquarters, are: Aisling Camps, Amir Taghi, Terrence Zhou of Bad Binch TongTong, Emily Dawn Long, Julia Ferentinos of Juju Vera, Jamie Haller, Zane Li of Lii, George Inaki of Milamore, Claire Sullivan of Miss Claire Sullivan, and Zoe Gustavia Anna Whalen. Uniquely, this year’s designers are all womenswear or gender inclusive, with the exception of two jewelry brands; however, all represent New York’s bustling creative scene. posts.caption: We have Jane Birkin to thank for making raffia synonymous with summer. Before she was associated with the leather Hermès bag that bears her name, the actor was known for accessorizing her sunny smile and flared jeans with a charming basket bag. The recent renaissance of raffia totes dates back to the spring 2021 season when they were seen on the runway at Loewe and The Row.  Looking to pull some lucky straw this summer? posts.caption: @Dior’s ties to La Serenissima run deep. Back in Christian Dior’s heyday, the couturier and Salvador Dali devised extravagant costumes for a legendary ball thrown by Charles de Bestegui at his palace, Palazzo Labia. This week, Dior's Casinò was back in play for Diorissima, a high jewelry homage to Venice—and art—courtesy of Victoire de Castellane. posts.caption: The best work bags for women should always meet two criteria: function and style. When searching for your own, ask yourself, is it durable? Will it be sizable enough to fit all of your essentials—laptop, notebooks, planners, wallet, keys, and beauty staples? Is it lightweight and comfortable enough to wear over your shoulders (and coat!), especially during a daily commute? At the link in our bio, shop the best work bags for women that will carry all of your essentials, no matter where you’re headed. Photographed by @jancrlo

posts.caption:
: A wedding takes months to plan, but a marriage lasts a lifetime. 💍✨
From finances and future goals to communication styles and expectations, the strongest relationships are built through conversations that happen long before the wedding day.
Before choosing the perfect venue, outfit, or décor, take time to understand each other's values, priorities, and vision for the future. Because while a beautiful wedding creates memories, a strong marriage creates a life together. ❤️
Save this post and share it with someone who's planning their forever.

✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
𝐇𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐲 𝐄𝐯𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐌𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐠𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐃𝐚𝐲.
To the people who have attended 1,000 weddings...
not to sit in the crowd, but to orchestrate the magic.
To the people who know every family’s story...
but still calm the room and say,
“𝐷𝑜𝑛’𝑡 𝑤𝑜𝑟𝑟𝑦, 𝑤𝑒’𝑙𝑙 ℎ𝑎𝑛𝑑𝑙𝑒 𝑖𝑡.”
To the people who survive on coffee, adrenaline, and impossible deadlines.
To the people who gracefully handle,
“𝐽𝑢𝑠𝑡 𝑜𝑛𝑒 𝑠𝑚𝑎𝑙𝑙 𝑐ℎ𝑎𝑛𝑔𝑒..?”
when everything is already beautifully set,
yet still make it happen flawlessly.
To the people who miss meals,
miss sleep,
and miss their own celebrations...
just so someone else’s special day becomes unforgettable.
You are not just event managers.
You are therapists.
Dream builders.
And somehow...
you do all of that with absolute grace.
Today, the spotlight belongs to the people who spend their lives making sure everyone else’s shine.
𝑯𝒂𝒑𝒑𝒚 𝑬𝒗𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝑴𝒂𝒏𝒂𝒈𝒆𝒓𝒔 𝑫𝒂𝒚.
Now answer honestly...
How many times have you looked a client in the eye, said “𝐸𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑦𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑖𝑠 𝑢𝑛𝑑𝑒𝑟 𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑡𝑟𝑜𝑙,” while you were still figuring things out, only to deliver absolute perfection at the end? 😅
#wvconnect #eventmanagersday

posts.caption:
Meet the Strategist Shaping the Future of the Industry
Vision, leadership, and strategic thinking are at the heart of every thriving industry. Few exemplify these qualities better than Dakshinaa Moorthy.
As CEO of WVHPL and Chief Strategem Officer at ICEI, Dakshinaa has been instrumental in building platforms, fostering partnerships, and creating opportunities that strengthen the wedding, events, hospitality, and experiential ecosystem.
Known for his sharp business acumen and forward-thinking approach, he has consistently worked behind the scenes to transform ideas into impactful industry initiatives. His ability to identify emerging opportunities, connect stakeholders, and drive meaningful growth has made him a respected leader across the sector.
With a deep understanding of both business strategy and industry dynamics, Dakshinaa continues to play a vital role in shaping conversations around collaboration, innovation, destination development, and the future of celebrations in India.
At Hindusthan 2.0, we are honoured to welcome a leader whose vision extends beyond events and towards building a stronger, more connected industry for the future.
Join Dakshinaa Moorthy and 300+ industry leaders as we gather in the heart of the Himalayas to explore new opportunities, build meaningful partnerships, and champion the Wed in India movement.
📍 Punarnava Resort, Mussoorie
📅 15–16 July 2026
Reserve your seat today.
📞 For details: 9829754321
#Hindusthan2_0 #ChaloUttarakhand #WedInUttarakhand #WedInIndia #ICEI

posts.caption:
@maudeapatow is ready now. Fresh off her return in the highly anticipated third season of #Euphoria, Apatow is stepping into a new chapter with the kind of clarity and control that only comes from growing up in real time—on-screen, off-screen, and alongside co-stars who have gone on to navigate intergalactic levels of fame and careers of their own. "Just thinking about us being little kids and having no idea what was in store to now… It's insane," she says.
In our May cover story, Apatow opens up about the roles that shaped her, the relationships that fulfilled her, the theater work that grounded her, and the rituals—travel, matcha, and all—that keep her moving forward. Read our full interview with the actress and director at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
@lilamoss is having the time of her life. At just 23, Moss has already graced the cover of British Vogue, starred in a @marcjacobs beauty campaign, and walked for @coperni, @versace, @miumiu, and @victoriassecret. But her path into fashion wasn’t always a given, despite having a supermodel mother. “I was always really interested in fashion,” she says. “I was always putting on my mom’s shoes and doing fake runways. But it was never something really that my mom wanted me to do.”
From her very first modeling opportunity, Moss knew she had found her lane. Now, she’s using her platform to advocate for causes close to her heart—from proudly wearing her type 1 diabetes tech in editorials to collaborating on the first-ever type 1 diabetic Barbie. See how Moss is carving her own path in our summer cover story at the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
Here comes the bride, all dressed in @Schiaparelli. For her intimate wedding ceremony in London over the weekend, @dualipa wore a couture wedding suit designed by @danielroseberry, a @Bvlgari Serpenti necklace, and an accessory that 2026 brides are ditching traditional veils for: a sculptural bridal hat. “The biggest trend this year is definitely hats for the courthouse. There’s lots of girls looking for pillboxes in particular,” Lizzie Wheeler, the founder behind vintage bridal shop @studiodorothy.nyc, told us. “Brides are taking an increasingly editorial approach to their weddings from all angles—that means an increase in head-to-toe looks,” Wheeler adds. For more on the bridal hat trend taking over 2026, head to the link in our bio.

posts.caption:
@jodiemcomer has never played it safe. ⁠
⁠
After captivating audiences as Villanelle in "Killing Eve," the actress is stepping into quieter, more complex territory with "The Death of Robin Hood." ⁠

posts.caption:
On Monday night, The @lgbtcenternyc kicked off the first day of Pride Month with its annual Fashion Centered fundraiser.⁠
⁠
The community center, based in the West Village, only closes its doors to host one private event onsite each year. With significant backing from the fashion industry, the Fashion Centered raises support for The Center’s broad resources and programming for LGBTQIA+ New Yorkers. This year’s event, which honored @thombrowne with The NYC LGBT Community Center’s Lifetime Achievement Award, was supported by presenting sponsor Pandora, as well as  Jean Paul Gaultier, Supreme  and Ferragamo.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: @ktauer⁠
Photos: @lexieblacklock/WWD

posts.caption:
The historic @neimanmarcus flagship in downtown Dallas will close for good on Sept. 30 this year, Saks Global, the parent company of Neiman Marcus, revealed Tuesday.⁠
⁠
The decision to close the location comes after negotiating for months upon the urging of the city of Dallas and local leaders to work out an arrangement for how the store could keep operating. Redeveloping the site with a downsized Neiman’s store and converting floors to galleries, an event space, even a theater, were considered at one point.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: David Moin

posts.caption:
Where are the “Sephora tweens” now?
More than two years after that term catapulted through the cultural lexicon as Gen Alpha’s first tweens entered the beauty industry — often in droves, with a particular penchant for prestige and in some cases, unsuitable, products — the hype has moderated.
Case in point: NIQ data shows that while facial skin care sales grew 26.7 percent among households with kids ages 13 to 17 in 2023 — the year the “Sephora tween” was coined — that growth has most recently leveled off to about 8.9 percent during the 12 months ending in April.
More meaningful habits, though, have emerged from the frenzy.
Read more details, including which brands and products teen girls are overindexing on today, on WWD.com. #linkinbio
Report: @noorlobaddie
📸: Getty Images, @bubble, @rhode

posts.caption:
@kering_official has named Gianfranco D’Attis the new chief executive officer of @alexandermcqueen, one of Kering’s underperforming brands, effective Wednesday.⁠
⁠
D’Attis, most recently the CEO of Prada, will be based in London and report to Kering CEO Luca de Meo.⁠
⁠
In a statement released Monday evening after the end of trading on the Paris Bourse, de Meo said D’Attis “brings a powerful combination of strategic vision, operational rigor and deep luxury expertise.”⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Miles Socha

posts.caption:
@loewe is kicking off its 180th birthday with a capsule collection, a frisky campaign, a special anniversary magazine and an animated film narrated by Spanish actor @antoniobanderas.⁠
⁠
The Madrid-based leather workshop, Loewe is billed as the second oldest luxury fashion house, with Banderas noting that it existed before the light bulb, the telephone and the moon landing.⁠
⁠
Key moments in the brand came in 1872, when a German merchant named Enrique Loewe Roessberg unified the artisans under his name; 1905, when Loewe became an official supplier to the Spanish crown, and 1988, when the Loewe Foundation was established.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Miles Socha
